Styles › Sustainable / Passive

2000s – present · Global

Sustainable / Passive

Eco-friendly, efficient, harmonious — green roofs and living façades.

  • Overview
  • Design Philosophy
  • Visual Characteristics
  • Materials
  • Color Palette
  • Façade Guide
  • Lighting
  • Building Types
  • Design Checklist
  • Common Mistakes
  • AI Prompts
  • Gallery
  • Related

01 — Overview

Overview

Definition. Sustainable / Green architecture makes environmental performance the design driver — natural ventilation, shading, green roofs and living façades shaping an exterior that works with climate and integrates planting.

Historical background. From early bioclimatic design to today's net-zero and biophilic towers, green architecture moved from niche to mainstream as climate concern grew.

Origin. Global; advanced by European and Asian practices.

Timeline

2000

Green-building rating systems spread

2014

Bosco Verticale — vertical forest towers

2019

CopenHill — waste-to-energy with public roof

2021

Net-zero and timber high-rise mainstream

Key Architects

Stefano BoeriBIGWOHAKen Yeang

Influential Projects

Bosco Verticale

Stefano Boeri · 2014
Milan, Italy

CopenHill

BIG · 2019
Copenhagen, Denmark

One Central Park

Jean Nouvel · 2014
Sydney, Australia

02 — Design Philosophy

Design Philosophy

Core Principles

Performance-led formNatural ventilation & shadingLiving façadesRenewable materialsHarmony with nature

The Thinking

The exterior is an environmental instrument — oriented, shaded, planted and ventilated so the building works with climate, lowers energy and brings nature back into the city.

Values It Represents

ResponsibilityEfficiencyWellbeingResilience

Why It Emerged

The climate imperative — architecture must reduce energy and carbon while improving human and ecological health.

03 — Visual Characteristics

Visual Characteristics

MassingShaped to sun, wind and view; terraced for planting and daylight.
Form LanguageClean contemporary volumes layered with greenery and shading.
CompositionPlanted terraces, brise-soleil and louvre systems articulating the façade.
ProportionHuman-scaled, with depth for planting and shade.
GeometryOrthogonal or gently sculptural, optimised by performance.
RhythmRepetitive shading fins, planters and balconies.
SymmetryUsually asymmetrical, driven by orientation.
ScaleHouse to high-rise; greenery at every scale.
Roof TypesGreen/accessible roofs, solar arrays, sky gardens.
Window ProportionOptimised glazing with deep shading and operable vents.
EntranceOpen, planted, daylit thresholds.
Façade ArticulationLiving/green façades, timber, recycled materials, brise-soleil and PV integrated.

04 — Materials

Materials

Greenery / planting

Living façades and roofs cool, clean air and reconnect nature.

Timber (sustainable)

Renewable, low-carbon structure and cladding.

Recycled materials

Lower embodied carbon and honest texture.

Glass (high-performance)

Daylight with thermal control.

05 — Color Palette

Color Palette

Natural, fresh, harmonious — greens and earth tones.

Primary

Foliage Green
#5a7048
Warm Timber
#b8915c

Accent

Earth Brown
#7a6a55
Sky Glass
#a6c0c8

Material Colors

Recycled Grey
#9b9183
Concrete
#b3aea2

Typical Combinations

  • +Green + timber + glass
  • +Earth tones with living façade

06 — Façade Design Guide

Façade Design Guide

Window CompositionOptimised glazing sized to orientation; operable for ventilation; deeply shaded.
EmphasisLayered depth — planters, balconies and shading fins.
Shading DevicesBrise-soleil, louvres, deep balconies and planting are central.
BalconiesDeep planted terraces as living, shading outdoor rooms.
FramesTimber or recycled-content; operable.
OpeningsGenerous but performance-tuned and screened.
Architectural DetailsIntegrated PV, planters, irrigation and louvre systems.
OrnamentationLiving greenery and shading rhythm provide all the richness.

07 — Lighting

Lighting

Natural Lighting Strategy

Daylight optimised and shaded; planting filters and softens light.

Artificial Lighting Concept

Low-energy, layered, warm; lit greenery at night.

Night Façade Appearance

Soft glow with illuminated living façades and terraces.

Recommendations

Efficient warm LED; light planting subtly; avoid skyglow; emphasise green layers.

08 — Typical Building Types

Typical Building Types

Where this style performs best:

Residential towersOfficesCampusesMixed-useCivic buildingsSchools

09 — Design Checklist

Design Checklist

Tap an item to mark it complete — saved to your browser.

  • Drive form from orientation, sun and wind
  • Integrate green roofs and living façades
  • Add brise-soleil and deep shading
  • Use renewable/recycled materials
  • Optimise and shade glazing; allow ventilation
  • Layer planted terraces for depth
  • Integrate PV and water systems
  • Keep a natural green-and-earth palette

10 — Common Mistakes

Common Mistakes

Elements that contradict the style — what to avoid:

  • ✕'Greenwashing' — token planting on a sealed glass box
  • ✕Unshaded glazing causing overheating
  • ✕Ignoring orientation and ventilation
  • ✕High-carbon materials undercutting the intent
  • ✕Planting with no irrigation/maintenance strategy

11 — AI Prompt Examples

AI Prompt Examples

Optimised for Midjourney, ChatGPT image tools or Claude — exterior renders. Tap copy to use.

Exterior

Sustainable green residential tower exterior, planted terraces and living façade, timber and recycled materials, brise-soleil shading, integrated solar, lush greenery, daylight, architectural photography --ar 2:3 --style raw

Façade Concept

Green façade, deep planted balconies, timber louvres, brise-soleil, high-performance glazing, abundant planting, frontal elevation, natural light

Night View

Sustainable building exterior at night, softly lit living façade and terraces, warm low-energy glow, greenery, architectural night photography

Aerial View

Aerial of a green building, green roofs and sky gardens, solar arrays, planted terraces, surrounding landscape, drone photography

Street Perspective

Street view of a sustainable building exterior, living green façade, planted entrance, timber shading, people, documentary photography, daylight

Masterplan

Sustainable district masterplan, green roofs, planted streets, solar, water systems, blue-green network, axonometric, green palette
